pages.title: Rite of Severance
pages.text: Not all who swear the Oath wish to bear it forever. For such cowards—or survivors—there exists one final answer: the $(l)Rite of Severance$(). This altar does not simply remove the brand. It tears it free.$(br2)The Rite destroys the $(item)Oathbound Relic$() utterly. Nothing of the bond remains but ash.

pages.title: The Altar
pages.text: Construct the altar and place it where you intend to be judged. It is not a gentle thing, nor a safe one.

pages.title: Requirements
pages.text: To begin the Rite, the bearer must still have the $(item)Oathbound Relic$() equipped.$(br2)The altar also demands:$(li)1 Oblivion Stone$(li)4 Echo Shards$(li)4 Crying Obsidian$(li)16 Soul Soil$(br2)The Rite may only begin $(thing)at night$() or within $(thing)The End$().

pages.title: What Happens
pages.text: Once awakened, the altar binds the bearer to itself for $(thing)60 seconds$(). The branded must remain within $(thing)7 blocks$() of the altar or the Rite fails.$(br2)Every few moments the altar forces the curses of the Oath back through the body: flame, weakness, hunger, darkness, shattered protection, and the rage of nearby creatures.

pages.title: Completion
pages.text: If the bearer survives, the Relic is $(bold)destroyed$(), the brand is $(bold)cleansed$(), and only a trace remains: $(item)Fractured Relic Ash$().$(br2)Should the bearer flee, die, or lose the Relic before the Rite ends, the Severance fails—and the Oath endures.
